ogTitle: Japoonvale vs Jardine River: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Japoonvale and Jardine River.
heading: Japoonvale vs Jardine River — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Japoonvale, Queensland and Jardine River, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Japoonvale has a lower overall climate risk score (33/100) compared to Jardine River (34/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Japoonvale or Jardine River safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Japoonvale has a flood risk score of 30/100, while Jardine River scores 41/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Japoonvale scores 64/100 for bushfire risk. Jardine River scores 54/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Japoonvale or Jardine River?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Japoonvale has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.58% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
